items.reason: MetricFlow has no categorical dimension hierarchy / rolls-up-to / finest-grain-resolution construct; granularity exists for time dimensions only.

Within a semantic model every dimension is queryable with every measure; dbt-semantic-interfaces 0.9.0 has no per-metric allowed/denied dimension field (see spec_field_inventory.json).

dropped_semantics.description: Unsupported because the public dataset has no cost or profit field.

items.reason: MetricFlow has no 'declared-but-unanswerable / refuse-with-reason' metric construct. Only encoding is omission from the YAML: the engine then returns a generic 'unknown metric' parse error indistinguishable from a typo, with no policy provenance.

No disclosure/aggregate-only policy construct. Encodable only by OMITTING the column from the semantic model (enforcement-by-absence): no policy object, no audit trail, generic 'not found' error; and omission also removes legitimate aggregate uses of the column (e.g. invoice_id as a declarable entity for joins).

No disclosure/aggregate-only policy construct. Encodable only by OMITTING the column from the semantic model (enforcement-by-absence): no policy object, no audit trail, generic 'not found' error; and omission also removes legitimate aggregate uses of the column (e.g. invoice_id as a declarable entity for joins).
No coverage/completeness declaration in the spec; out-of-coverage time windows return an empty result with exit code 0 instead of a coverage-based refusal (probe P9).

dropped_semantics.as_of: bitemporal as-of anchoring of metric definitions

items.reason: mf query exposes only --start-time/--end-time filters on metric_time; no as-of flag, no bitemporal construct (probe P8).
All rejections surface as CLI error text from query parsing; there is no structured refusal object and no reason-code taxonomy.
